excerpt: Hypocrites and wicked figures who secretly cast doubts in believers' hearts to mislead them, though appearing as friends.
When the sūra declares 'Refuge in the true God of mankind,' it points toward the station of the Qāʾim, whose station represents the highest invocation of the divine name.
The 'evil of the sneaking whisperer' refers to those hidden enemies—hypocrites and the wicked whose doubts lodge secretly in believers' hearts.
source_location_hint: Commentary on Sura al-Nās verse on waswāsa

excerpt: Spiritual death is attained by purifying through the water of religious knowledge and donning the white garment of piety.
Hell is first glimpsed beyond the veil through knowledge by report; after death, the eye beholds these hells and certainty settles upon him.
source_location_hint: Discourse on two modes of witnessing Hell

excerpt: Amīr al-Muʾminīn ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib and his descendants, the Imāms of their age, are al-Na'īm; those who stand against them are al-Jahīm.
These two fruits are the fruits of Paradise—no matter how much you take from them, they never diminish. They resemble a lamp; by its light you may kindle lamp after lamp.
Adam emerged from dust without preceding conception like the fig, created without natural preconditions. The fig also alludes to the Mahdī who shall appear suddenly without prior signs.
Jesus Christ presented the truths, meanings, and summaries of the Torah revealed to Moses. God makes a subtle reference to these truths in the verse about the olive yielding oil.
source_location_hint: Interpretation of Mount Sinai and the tree yielding oil

excerpt: God made pilgrimage obligatory upon His first house and made it a sign for His final Prophet, itself a sign of the Resurrection. Prophethood and Resurrection were sent together.
source_location_hint: Commentary on Bilād al-Amīn (The Secure City)

excerpt: The organs of religion—the servants of religion—each perform their own work. The Mustajīb acts like the tooth, cutting away immorality of beliefs. The Mazūnīn provide knowledge as the tongue provides saliva.
source_location_hint: Commentary on Sura al-Nāziʿāt (Those Who Tear Out)

excerpt: One of Mawlānā ʿAlī's titles is al-ʿArīḍ al-Bāṭin: intrinsically, there is no position above you and you are the greatest scholar of inner knowledge.
The Prophet said: 'I have taken from five, given to five, and for me and my Lord, there are five.' This indicates the orientation of religion through hierarchical transmission.
source_location_hint: Hadith on the Prophet's knowledge transmission

excerpt: The spiritual treasures were given to Mawlānā Aḥmad from the station of Muḥammad, alluding to the gifts Muḥammad Muṣṭafā bestowed upon Mawlānā Fāṭimah.
source_location_hint: Commentary on Sura al-Qadr (The Night of Decree)

excerpt: The month refers to the Imām and his thirty witnesses; each day is a witness. The Imām gathers all thirty Witnesses as the month gathers all thirty days.
source_location_hint: Taʾwīl of temporal and hierarchical structure in Sura al-Qadr

excerpt: There are two types of angels: the Cherubim (Nāsūt—the Human) and the Close Ones (Lāhūt—the Divine). Both components descend upon this night.
Every Muḥammad always receives from Muḥammad, and every ʿAlī from ʿAlī. This continuous process of transmission persists throughout the periods of divine guidance.
